ncs_standard_definition.description_short: A Drafter creates detailed technical drawings and documents from specifications, blueprints, and other data to visualize and communicate engineering concepts.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Drafter's primary responsibility is to create accurate and precise technical drawings, models, and documents using computer-aided design (CAD) software and other tools. They work closely with engineers, architects, and other stakeholders to ensure that their designs meet the required specifications and standards.

first_stepper.description: Standard route for a high school student.
target_degrees: B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering or related field

choice_maker.description: Has a generic degree, needs a bridge.
acceptable_base_degrees: B.Tech in Computer Science or related field
certifications_or_bridge_needed: Certified Drafter (CD) or Certified CAD Technician (CCT)

future_builder.description: Already in the sector, looking to advance.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2 years as Junior Drafter or related role
advanced_skills_to_learn: Advanced CAD software like SolidWorks or Autodesk Inventor

problem_solver_uturn.description: Switching from an unrelated field. No degree required.
portfolio_proof_required: Portfolio of technical drawings or models created using CAD software

progression.senior_level: Lead Drafter or Drafter Manager (5+ yrs)

roadmap.title: Capstone & Portfolio
topics: Create a portfolio of technical drawings or models
